home.title: Toolbox
home.tagline: A small set of personal frontend tools — everything runs in the browser.

palette.title: Search tools
palette.description: Find by tool name or description

network.name: Network
network.description: IP / DNS / subnet (with public API queries)

jwt.name: JWT
jwt.description: Decode, sign, verify JSON Web Token

hash.name: Hash
hash.description: MD5 / SHA-1 / SHA-256 / SHA-384 / SHA-512 at once

timestamp.name: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Timestamp ↔ date (Unix / ISO / RFC) + multi-zone clock, compose, batch

color.name: Color
color.description: HEX / RGB / HSL / OKLCH + WCAG contrast + palettes

number-base.name: Number Base
number-base.description: Integer base conversion (BigInt) + bit grid + bitwise ops

uuid.name: UUID
uuid.description: UUID v1 / v3 / v4 / v5 / v7 + decode (version, variant, timestamp)

qr-code.name: QR Code
qr-code.description: QR for URL / WiFi / vCard / Email / SMS / Geo (SVG / PNG, local)

lorem.name: Lorem Ipsum
lorem.description: Placeholder text — Lorem / Hipster / Pirate / Bacon themes

regex.name: Regex Tester
regex.description: Regex match, capture groups, replace preview

dns.name: DNS Lookup
dns.description: DNS query (Cloudflare DoH, click to trigger)

media.name: Media
media.description: Clip, concat, extract audio, convert (ffmpeg.wasm, local)

image-editor.name: Image Editor
image-editor.description: Crop / rotate / filter / annotate, with project save/reload

json.description: Format, minify, validate JSON. All processing runs locally in the browser.

xml.description: Format, minify, validate XML. Uses the browser's native DOMParser (no third-party dep).

yaml.description: Convert between YAML and JSON. Powered by js-yaml, supporting YAML 1.2 subset.

base64.description: Base64 encode/decode with UTF-8 and URL-safe (-_) variants. All processing runs locally.

url.description: URL encode/decode. Component mode (encodeURIComponent) escapes all reserved characters; URI mode (encodeURI) preserves :/?&#= etc.

hex.description: Text ↔ hexadecimal bytes (UTF-8). Decode tolerates space / colon / comma / dash / underscore separators.

html-entity.description: HTML entity encode/decode. Encode uses the minimal safe set (& < > " '); decode supports all named + numeric entities (decimal / hex).

jwt.description: Decode and verify JSON Web Token. All processing runs locally.

claim.iss: Issuer — identifies the principal that issued the JWT (RFC 7519 §4.1.1)
claim.sub: Subject — identifies the principal the JWT is about (RFC 7519 §4.1.2)
claim.exp: Expiration Time — Unix seconds; the JWT must not be accepted after this (RFC 7519 §4.1.4)
claim.nbf: Not Before — Unix seconds; the JWT must not be accepted before this (RFC 7519 §4.1.5)
claim.iat: Issued At — Unix seconds when the JWT was issued (RFC 7519 §4.1.6)
claim.jti: JWT ID — unique identifier; prevents replay (RFC 7519 §4.1.7)
claim.name: Display name (OpenID Connect)
claim.auth_time: Authentication time — Unix seconds when end-user auth occurred (OIDC)
claim.azp: Authorized Party — OAuth client ID the token was issued to (OIDC)
claim.nonce: Nonce — replay-protection value bound to the auth request (OIDC)

headerField.alg: Algorithm used to sign the JWT (RFC 7515 §4.1.1, e.g. HS256, RS256, ES256)
headerField.kid: Key ID — hint indicating which key was used (RFC 7515 §4.1.4)
headerField.cty: Content Type — payload type for nested JWTs (RFC 7515 §4.1.10)
headerField.jku: JWK Set URL — URL pointing to a set of JSON Web Keys (RFC 7515 §4.1.2)
headerField.jwk: JSON Web Key — public key in JWK format embedded in the header (RFC 7515 §4.1.3)
headerField.x5u: X.509 URL — URL to the X.509 cert chain (RFC 7515 §4.1.5)
headerField.x5c: X.509 Cert Chain — embedded X.509 chain (RFC 7515 §4.1.6)

jwt.secretPlaceholderPrivate:
-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----
...
-----END PRIVATE KEY-----

or JWK: { "kty": "RSA", ... }
jwt.secretPlaceholderPublic:
-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----
...
-----END PUBLIC KEY-----

or JWK: { "kty": "RSA", ... }
jwt.statusNoSig: No signature key — token uses placeholder
jwt.needPrivateKey: A private key is required to sign

hash.description: Produce MD5 / SHA-1 / SHA-256 / SHA-384 / SHA-512 simultaneously. SHA uses native Web Crypto; MD5 uses js-md5 (**do not use for security**).

hmac.description: HMAC based on Web Crypto (SHA-1 / 256 / 384 / 512). Key stays in the browser.

timestamp.description: Convert between timestamps and dates. Supports Unix seconds / milliseconds / ISO 8601 / RFC 2822. Includes a discrete-fields composer, batch conversion, and Windows FILETIME / Excel-serial outputs.
timestamp.cannotCompose: Cannot compose timestamp from these fields

color.description: Color formats: HEX / RGB / HSL / OKLCH. Edit any field, slider or the picker to sync everything. Includes WCAG contrast check and palette generators.
color.contrastSample: Sample text · The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog

numberBase.description: Integer base conversion. Backed by BigInt — accepts 0x / 0b / 0o prefixes, supports two's-complement display and bitwise ops at 8/16/32/64-bit widths.
numberBase.opsHint: AND / OR / XOR take a 2nd operand · SHL / SHR use it as shift amount · NOT inverts within width

csvJson.description: Convert between CSV and JSON. First row of CSV becomes keys; JSON must be an array of objects. Handles quoted fields, commas / newlines inside fields, and escaped quotes.
csvJson.errRootArray: Root must be an array (array of objects)
csvJson.errRowObject: Each array element must be an object

uuid.description: Generate UUIDs (v1 / v3 / v4 / v5 / v7) and decode any UUID's version, variant and embedded timestamp.
uuid.name: Name

password.description: Password generator backed by crypto.getRandomValues (CSPRNG, no modulo bias). Tabs for a random string or a Diceware-style passphrase.

qr.description: Generate a QR code (SVG / PNG). Rendered locally; content never leaves your browser. Type tabs compose URL / WiFi / vCard / Email / SMS / GeoURI payloads.
qr.placeholder: Type content to generate a QR code…

lorem.description: Placeholder text generator. Output by paragraph / sentence / word count; pick a theme and optionally wrap as HTML.
lorem.startWithLorem: Start with "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet…"

diff.description: Text diff (line or word granularity). All computation is local.

case.description: Convert between common naming styles: camelCase / PascalCase / snake_case / kebab-case / Title Case etc.
case.title: Title Case

sortDedupe.description: Per-line processing: sort, dedupe, trim, ignore case.

regex.description: Based on the browser's native RegExp. Supports every ECMAScript flag.

ipInfo.description: IP lookup. Only makes a request to ipapi.co on click (free tier 1k/day/IP).
ipInfo.inputPlaceholder: Leave blank to look up your own IP, or enter IPv4 / IPv6
ipInfo.empty: Click <1>Lookup</1> or <3>Look up my IP</3> to start

dns.description: Based on Cloudflare DoH (cloudflare-dns.com). Only queries on click.
dns.empty: Enter a domain + record type, then click <1>Lookup</1>

imageEditor.description: Local image editor — layers, masks, brush/eraser, adjustments, annotations, undo/redo. Save a project file (.json) to resume editing later.
imageEditor.dropTitle: Drop an image (PNG / JPEG / WebP / GIF), or click to pick
imageEditor.dropSub: All processing runs in your browser; nothing is uploaded. You can also drop a previously-saved .json project to resume.

adjustments.invertHint: Inverts every pixel — no parameters. Apply to commit it as a layer.
adjustments.curvesHint: Click to add a point, drag to move. Alt-click an interior point to remove. Endpoints are locked to x=0/255.

filters.despeckleHint: Reduces speckle noise via a 3×3 median per channel. No parameters — Apply to commit as a layer.
filters.findEdgesHint: Highlights edges via the Sobel operator (white background, dark edges). No parameters — Apply to commit as a layer.

editMenu.cutNoBackground: Can't cut from background — duplicate the layer first
editMenu.cutNotSupported: Cut isn't supported on this layer type

layerMenu.mergeDownNoBelow: Nothing below this layer to merge with
layerMenu.stamped: Visible layers stamped to new layer

rotateArbitrary.title: Rotate Image
rotateArbitrary.hint: Rotation is destructive; layers / transforms / crop are flattened and reset.

clippingMask.created: Clipping mask created — layer masked to layer below
clippingMask.unsupportedKind: Clipping mask only applies to shape, Smart Object, and group layers.

quickMask.entered: Quick Mask mode — paint with brush (black removes from selection, white adds). Press Q to exit.
quickMask.exited: Quick Mask exited — selection updated to mask bbox
quickMask.exitedNoSel: Quick Mask exited — mask wasn't loaded, selection unchanged
quickMask.exitedEmpty: Quick Mask exited — no selected pixels

maskActions.noMaskToRemove: No mask on the selected layer.
maskActions.applied: Mask applied — baked into the layer below
maskActions.applyOnlyMask: Apply Mask works on a Mask layer.
maskActions.applyEmptyMask: Mask is empty — nothing to apply.
maskActions.applyNoTarget: No layer beneath this mask to apply it to.
maskActions.applyTargetUnsupported: Apply Mask needs an image / shape / Smart Object below — adjustment, filter, and mask layers can't be baked.

adjMask.added: Mask added to layer — paint with the brush to gate where the effect applies
adjMask.alreadyHas: This layer already has an adjustment mask.
adjMask.unsupportedKind: Adjustment masks apply only to adjustment / filter layers.

maskRaster.created: Raster mask layer added — paint with the brush (black hides, white reveals)
maskRaster.converted: Mask converted to raster — paintable with the brush
maskRaster.notAMask: Convert to Raster only works on a Mask layer.
maskRaster.alreadyRaster: This mask is already a raster — nothing to convert.

freeTransform.useSOHandles: Use the corner / rotation handles on this Smart Object to transform.
freeTransform.onlySO: Free Transform currently applies to Smart Objects — convert this layer first.

imageMenu.trimNothing: Nothing to trim — image has no transparent border.
imageMenu.trimNeedsRevealAll: Clear the current crop (Reveal All) before trimming.

smartObject.title: Smart Object
smartObject.unsupportedKind: Mask / adjustment / filter layers can't be converted to Smart Objects.
smartObject.alreadySO: Layer is already a Smart Object.
smartObject.noBbox: Layer has no measurable bounds — can't convert.
smartObject.rasterFailed: Failed to rasterize the layer for the Smart Object source.

imageEditor.panelStubChannels: Channels panel — coming soon (per-channel R/G/B/A view).
imageEditor.panelStubPaths: Paths panel — coming soon (vector paths from the Pen tool).
imageEditor.panelStubInfo: Info panel — coming soon (live cursor color/coords).

channels.readOnlyHint: Read-only preview. Alpha = white on fully opaque images (not a bug). Per-channel editing is a v2 feature.

paths.empty: No vector paths yet. Use the Pen tool to draw one.
paths.makeWorkPathHint: Convert the active selection into a closed Pen path layer.
paths.makeSelectionHint: Use the selected path's outline as the new selection.
paths.noSelection: No active selection — make one first.
paths.emptyPath: Path has too few anchors to make a selection.

history.empty: No history yet — make an edit to start recording steps.

layerComps.title: Layer Comps
layerComps.empty: No comps saved. Capture the current layer state as a comp.
layerComps.applyHint: Restore layers + image to this comp

actions.recordStarted: Recording started — every edit becomes a step
actions.recordEmpty: Recording ended with no edits — nothing to save
actions.empty: No actions yet. Save a snapshot or start a recording.

brushes.customHint: Built-in presets. Custom brush presets are v2.
brushes.emptyCustom: Save the current brush settings as a preset.
brushes.importTipHint: Import an image file as a brush tip stamp (PNG with transparency works best).

toolHint.marquee: Drag a rectangular selection. Shift = add, Alt = subtract.
toolHint.lasso: Drag a freeform selection. Release to close.
toolHint.polyLasso: Click each vertex; click near start to close.
toolHint.wand: Click a region; tolerance controls how loose the match is.
toolHint.crop: Drag a region, then drag handles to resize. Enter = apply.
toolHint.eyedropper: Click anywhere to set the foreground colour.
toolHint.spotHeal: Drag over blemishes to clone surrounding pixels.
toolHint.stamp: Alt-click to set source, then drag to clone.
toolHint.historyBrush: Drag to paint pixels back from the initial image.
toolHint.brush: Drag to paint. Use [ ] to change size; Brushes panel for presets.
toolHint.eraser: Drag to erase pixels (or selection mask in mask mode).
toolHint.gradient: Drag from start to end colour.
toolHint.bucket: Click to fill connected pixels within tolerance.
toolHint.blur: Drag a region; pixels inside are blurred.
toolHint.dodge: Drag to brighten. Build-up: repeat strokes.
toolHint.burn: Drag to darken. Build-up: repeat strokes.
toolHint.pen: Click to add corner anchors; click-drag for smooth curves.
toolHint.arrowPath: Direct Selection — drag path anchors to reshape.
toolHint.text: Click to add a text layer.
toolHint.mosaic: Drag a region; pixels are pixelated.
toolHint.mask: Drag rects to define visible regions for layers below.
toolHint.zoom: Click to zoom in 2×; Alt-click to zoom out.
toolHint.hand: Drag to pan the workspace (Space also works).

imageEditor.moveToolHint: Move (V): drag layers; Cmd/Ctrl+J duplicate; Delete remove.
imageEditor.zoomToolHint: Zoom (Z): click to zoom in 2×, Alt-click to zoom out. Cmd/Ctrl+wheel zooms at cursor.
imageEditor.eyedropperHint: Eyedropper (I): click anywhere on the canvas to set the foreground color.
imageEditor.bucketHint: Paint Bucket (G): click to flood-fill connected pixels within tolerance. Result lands as a new layer.
imageEditor.bucketEmpty: Nothing to fill at that point — try a higher tolerance.
imageEditor.errBucketRead: Couldn't read pixels for fill (canvas tainted?).
imageEditor.gradientHint: Gradient (G): drag from start to end. Foreground at the start, background at the end. Result lands as a new layer.
imageEditor.blurHint: Blur: drag a rect on the canvas — the area inside gets blurred at render time. Adjustable via the layer's bounds; default radius is 8 px.
imageEditor.dodgeHint: Dodge (O): drag to brighten — like PS, repeat strokes to build up the effect. Layer opacity controls intensity.
imageEditor.burnHint: Burn: drag to darken. Repeat strokes to build up; layer opacity controls intensity.
imageEditor.marqueeHint: Marquee (M): drag a rectangular selection. Cmd/Ctrl+A select all · Cmd/Ctrl+D deselect.
imageEditor.lassoHint: Lasso (L): drag to trace a freeform region. Release the mouse to close and commit.
imageEditor.polyLassoHint: Polygonal Lasso: click to add vertices, click near the first vertex (or hit Esc to cancel) to close.
imageEditor.wandHint: Magic Wand (W): click a region — pixels within tolerance of the click colour are selected (bounding box).
imageEditor.wandEmpty: No matching region at that point — try a higher tolerance.
imageEditor.rotateViewHint: Rotate View (R): each click rotates the workspace 90°. Pixels are not modified — purely a viewing aid.
imageEditor.textToolHint: Type (T): click on the canvas to add text.
imageEditor.toolStubHint: {{tool}} is a placeholder — the palette button is here for parity with Photoshop, but the tool isn't implemented yet.
imageEditor.statusTip: Tool: {{tool}} · Hold Space to pan, Z for zoom tool
imageEditor.cropPendingHint: Press Enter to apply, Esc to cancel
imageEditor.dropImageHint: Drop image to add as a new layer
imageEditor.toolHint: Drawing: {{tool}} — drag on the canvas; click the same tool again to deselect
imageEditor.focusEnterHint: Fullscreen — hide the toolbox chrome and give the canvas the full viewport
imageEditor.handHint: Hand (H): drag to pan the canvas. Same as holding Space with any other tool.
imageEditor.noteHint: Note (N): click to drop a sticky-note marker. Notes show on screen but never bake into export.
imageEditor.frameHint: Frame: drag to draw a rectangular placeholder frame.
imageEditor.arrowPathHint: Path Selection (A): click to select a vector layer, drag to move. Behaves like the Move tool — full vector-anchor selection arrives with the Pen tool.
imageEditor.penHint: Pen (P): click to add corner anchors, click+drag to add smooth anchors with handles. Click the first anchor to close · Enter to commit open · Esc to cancel.
imageEditor.spotHealHint: Spot Healing (J): drag over a blemish — patches sampled from a nearby area are stamped along the stroke (offset locks at stroke start, mirrors if it would land out of bounds).
imageEditor.cloneStampHint: Clone Stamp (S): Alt-click to set the source point, then drag to paint pixels from there. Crosshair shows the active source.
imageEditor.cloneNeedSource: Alt-click first to set a source point
imageEditor.historyBrushHint: History Brush (Y): drag to paint back the original image — strips annotations along the stroke (transforms + adjustments still apply).

layers.dropOutside: Drop here to move out of group

layerStyle.title: Layer Style
layerStyle.unsupportedKind: Layer Style only applies to shape layers, groups, and Smart Objects.
layerStyle.pickFromList: Tick an effect on the left to configure it.
layerStyle.colorOverlayHint: Fills the layer's alpha with the chosen colour at the given opacity / blend.
layerStyle.patternHint: No pattern selected — falls back to a built-in checker tile.

imageEditor.autosaveAvailable: Unsaved work from {{name}} ({{ago}}) is available to restore.
imageEditor.textInlinePrompt: Type — ⌘Enter to commit, Esc to cancel

cidr.description: IPv4 subnet math: network / broadcast / mask / usable range / host count.

dnsType.CNAME: Canonical Name — alias to another domain (RFC 1035 §3.3.1)
dnsType.MX: Mail Exchanger — mail server for the domain (RFC 1035 §3.3.9)
dnsType.SOA: Start of Authority — zone metadata, primary NS, refresh interval (RFC 1035 §3.3.13)
dnsType.CAA: Certification Authority Authorization — which CAs may issue certs (RFC 8659)

regexFlag.g: global — find all matches, not only the first (sets RegExp.lastIndex on every exec)
regexFlag.m: multiline — ^ and $ match start/end of each line, not whole input
regexFlag.u: unicode — proper Unicode handling, surrogate pairs and Unicode property escapes (ES2015)
regexFlag.y: sticky — match only from RegExp.lastIndex; does not advance otherwise (ES2015)

hmacAlg.SHA-1: HMAC-SHA1 — 160-bit signature (RFC 2104 + FIPS 180-4); deprecated for new use

hashAlg.MD5: MD5 — 128-bit digest (RFC 1321). Broken: collision attacks are practical. Do NOT use for security.
hashAlg.SHA-1: SHA-1 — 160-bit digest (FIPS 180-4). Deprecated for new use; use SHA-256+.

media.description: Trim, concat, extract audio, convert. Powered by ffmpeg.wasm — all processing runs locally.
media.dropZoneTitle: Drag audio / video files here, or click to pick
media.dropZoneSub: All processing runs locally; nothing is uploaded
media.trimDescription: Trim the in → out range of the selected file. Uses `-c copy` (no re-encode) — fast, but cuts snap to the nearest keyframe.
media.concatDescription: Concatenate every file in the current order.
media.concatWarning: All files must share the same container and codec; in/out marks are ignored (trim each file first if needed).
media.audioDescription: Extract the entire audio track of the selected file.
media.convertDescription: Convert the selected file's format. Re-encodes — **may be slow** (tens of seconds to minutes depending on length).
media.selectionNone: No file selected — click a card above to select
media.loadingCore: Loading ffmpeg.wasm (~30 MB on first load, instant after)…
media.errOutAfterIn: Out must be greater than in
media.errMixedExt: All files must share the same extension (concat demuxer requires matching container & codec)
media.errConcat: Concat failed: usually caused by mismatched encoding/container. Convert to a uniform format first.
